title: Time Tracking based on Status change should track the time for the user which changed status
excerpt:
Hello 
At the moment, if you create an automation for start/stop time tracking based on status change. The user which has created the automation for the board is the assigned user for the time tracked. This makes in most…

posts.author: avongunten (Andreas Von Gunten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello

At the moment, if you create an automation for start/stop time tracking based on status change. The user which has created the automation for the board is the assigned user for the time tracked. This makes in most cases no sense. It should be the use who changes the status which triggers the automation to start/stop the time tracking.

Best
Andreas

posts.author: rosanacamara (Rosana Rodrigues Camara)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. I have the same problem. The recording of hours is not linked to the person responsible for the task, who was the one who changed the status and actually worked on the item. Isn’t that wrong? If it is manual it will be in the name of the person in charge and if it is automatic, will it not? It doesn’t make sense to be that way.
